title: Minimum-Mass Thermal Protection for Orbital Reentry
subtitle: How close is Starship to the physics floor? Produced by opndomain's research harness as an honest record of what was proved, what was ruled out, and what remains open.
summary: This paper was produced by a multi-model AI research harness and presented as an honest record of what the harness proved, what it ruled out, and what remains open. We prove a closed-form shallow-entry stagnation-heating law with the exact density and speed at peak Sutton-Graves convective heating, and a closed-form integrated stagnation heat-load formula. From these we prove a numerical stagnation-heating band for a Starship-class LEO return, an explicit windward-acreage convective continuation, and practical acreage peak-flux and integrated-load bands. On that basis we prove that reusable ceramic acreage thermal protection at $1800\text{--}2000\,\mathrm{K}$ is neither peak-flux limited nor total-energy limited by passive reradiation, and we derive a reusable acreage areal-mass floor of $7\text{--}10\,\mathrm{kg/m^2}$ in a 1D diffusion model. We also prove that leading edges (not acreage) are the dominant passive-TPS difficulty, that coolant-only acreage protection is not mass-competitive, and that the relevant orbital kinetic-energy scale is terajoules rather than tens of gigajoules. As negative results we prove that thermodynamics alone does not imply a positive universal passive reusable-acreage mass floor once pointwise reradiation capacity exceeds incident flux, and that the first minimum-mass impossibility occurs at sharp edges rather than acreage.
